title: DUMB MONEY
logline: A financial educator and retail investor becomes the unlikely leader of a grassroots movement when his YouTube analysis of GameStop stock triggers a short squeeze that pits everyday traders against Wall Street hedge funds, culminating in congressional testimony and a cultural reckoning over market manipulation.
antagonistOrConflict: Wall Street hedge funds (Gabe Plotkin/Melvin Capital, Steve Cohen, Ken Griffin) and the systemic inequality of the financial system
tone: Darkly comedic, populist, urgent, with moments of genuine pathos and righteous anger
themes: The democratization of finance vs. institutional gatekeeping
The power of collective action in the digital age
The American Dream deferred for working-class families

actBreaks.description: Keith visits his father's grave on Christmas, marking the emotional stakes before the squeeze begins in earnest and the story shifts from setup to escalation.

validation.ruling: Scene #46 (p47, 33.6%) is a powerful act break: the Gill family stands at Sara's grave in heavy silence, Keith's father asks about betting on the Patriots game, and Keith responds with their truism 'Always bet your heart'—this irreversible emotional moment fundamentally shifts Keith's motivation from casual trading to grief-driven obsession, forcing him into higher-stakes conflict as he walks away turning his phone over in his palm, contemplating his next move.

actBreaks.description: Keith is served legal papers and becomes a public figure, transforming from anonymous YouTuber to the face of the movement as he prepares for congressional testimony.

validation.ruling: Scene #109 (p101, 72.1%) is the definitive Act 2 break: Vlad learns the NSCC demands $3 billion to clear trades—more than Robinhood has ever raised—and realizes they'll be shut down if they can't pay. This irreversible crisis fundamentally changes the story's direction, forcing all protagonists into new, desperate conflict as the system itself threatens to collapse, raising stakes to their maximum before the final act.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script employs a mosaic structure that cross-cuts between five parallel storylines (Keith, Gabe, Jenny, Harmony/Riri, Marcos) to create a class-conscious ensemble piece. The first act establishes the economic disparity through visual and tonal contrasts—Gabe's Miami mansions vs. Keith's Brockton basement. Act Two accelerates through the short squeeze with rapid-fire montages and real news footage, building to the Robinhood trading halt that galvanizes the retail traders. Act Three pivots to consequences: financial, legal, and personal reckonings culminating in the congressional hearing. The structure mirrors the stock chart itself—slow accumulation, explosive volatility, uncertain resolution.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The script maintains relentless momentum through extremely short scenes (1.3 pages average) and aggressive intercutting. The opening 12 scenes establish all major players in just 8 pages. Mid-script (scenes 65-86), the pace becomes frenetic with single-location vignettes and montages as GME explodes. The hearing sequence (scenes 128-156) slows deliberately, allowing Keith's testimony to land with emotional weight. Notable rhythm shifts: the Christmas dinner argument (scene 97) stretches to provide character depth, while the trading frenzy (scenes 74-87) uses staccato cuts to mirror market chaos.

incitingIncident.description: Keith meets his friend Briggsy at a bar, orders a cheap Hamm's beer instead of Heineken, and deflects Briggsy's questions about his sister Sara's death, saying he doesn't want to talk about it.

validation.ruling: Scene #13 is merely a transition showing Keith riding the train, not an event that shatters his world. Scene #15 (p9, 6%) is the true inciting incident: Keith meets Briggsy at a bar and deflects questions about his sister Sara's death, establishing the emotional wound and timing (July 2020, just after her June death per scene #46) that catalyzes his obsessive GameStop investment as a coping mechanism for grief he refuses to process.

midpoint.description: Keith sits in front of a GME stock chart clutching a stuffed kitty and tells Caroline that the short squeeze is happening, then a supercut shows news coverage of GameStop soaring to record highs as retail traders wield unprecedented power.
midpoint.sceneHeading: INT. KEITH’S HOME - BASEMENT - BROCKTON, MA - DAY

validation.ruling: Scene #84 (p74, 52.9%) perfectly hits midpoint criteria: Keith clutches a stuffed kitty and declares 'The short squeeze. It's happening,' followed by a supercut of news coverage showing GME rocketing up on January 25. This is the point of no return where Keith shifts from reactive speculation to proactive vindication, stakes become intensely personal (his thesis proven on national TV), and he experiences a false victory before the collapse that follows.

climax.description: Keith and Kevin race naked on a high school track at night as title cards reveal Keith's final post was worth $34 million and he has retreated from public life.
climax.sceneHeading: EXT. HIGH SCHOOL TRACK - BROCKTON, MA - NIGHT

validation.ruling: Scene #136 is merely setup (Ken receives a subpoena), not the climax itself. Scene #161 (p140, 100%) is the true climax: Keith returns to the high school track at night where the story began, completing his character arc by confronting his grief and demonstrating the lessons learned through the entire GameStop saga—this is the final, defining moment that resolves his internal journey.

scenes.heading: INT. 6342 NORTH BAY ROAD - MIAMI, FL - DAY - SAME
scenes.summary: Gabe Plotkin strolls through his empty Miami mansion on a call with a lawyer, frustrated about delays in closing on a property he wants to demolish for a tennis court.

scenes.heading: INT. MAHOGANY-PANELED STUDY - GREENWICH - DAY
scenes.summary: Steve Cohen receives a massage in his study while on the phone with Gabe, informing him that GameStop stock has crossed a hundred dollars and retail traders are holding.

scenes.heading: INT. 6342 NORTH BAY ROAD - MIAMI, FL - DAY - SAME
scenes.summary: Gabe freezes in his living room upon hearing the news, hangs up with Steve, and starts running across his backyard.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGH SCHOOL TRACK - BROCKTON, MA - DAY - SAME
scenes.summary: Keith Gill runs fast on a high school track in Brockton, wearing a cat t-shirt and red sweatband, squinting up into the stands.

scenes.heading: EXT. GABE’S MODERN MANSION - MIAMI, FL - DAY - SAME
scenes.summary: Gabe, breathless and sweating, jumps over a fence into his neighboring modern mansion and runs through the front door.

scenes.heading: INT. GABE’S MODERN MANSION - OFFICE - MIAMI, FL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gabe rushes to his office, opens his Bloomberg terminal, and stares powerless at the GameStop stock chart climbing past one hundred dollars, sweating and jittery.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGH SCHOOL TRACK - BROCKTON, MA - DAY - SAME
scenes.summary: Keith rounds the corner of the track and picks up his pace to a sprint while maintaining steady, controlled breathing.

scenes.heading: INT. GABE’S MODERN MANSION - OFFICE - MIAMI, FL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gabe hyperventilates and explodes in fury at his lawyer over the phone about the delayed tennis court demolition, then hangs up as the phone buzzes again.

scenes.heading: INT. FOUR SEASONS - DINING ROOM - PALM BEACH, FL - DAY - SAME
scenes.summary: Ken Griffin calls Gabe from the Four Seasons while eating, and Gabe, mortified at his outburst, apologizes before being interrupted by another call from Steve Cohen.

scenes.heading: INT. MAHOGANY-PANELED STUDY - GREENWICH - DAY - SAME
scenes.summary: Steve tells Gabe that one guy appears to be driving all the GameStop buying and asks what guy Gabe is referring to.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGH SCHOOL TRACK - BROCKTON, MA - DAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Keith stops running, removes his sweatband, shakes out his limbs, sets a four-minute timer on his watch, crouches at the starting line, and takes off at high speed.

scenes.heading: INT. MAHOGANY-PANELED STUDY - GREENWICH - DAY - SAME
scenes.summary: Steve tells Gabe the trader goes by Roaring Kitty on YouTube and Deep Fucking Value on Reddit, then googles him and pulls up a video showing Keith's grinning face with a red sweatband.
scenes.turningPointNote: The hedge fund antagonists identify Keith as the driver behind GameStop's rise, establishing the central conflict.

scenes.heading: INT. THE T - MOVING - BOSTON, MA - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Keith rides the T into downtown Boston in July 2020, jittery and nervous, unable to sit still as he looks out at the city.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE T - BACK BAY STATION - BOSTON, MA - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Keith hops off the train at Back Bay station and dashes out, running rather than walking.

scenes.heading: EXT. BAR - BOSTON, MA - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Keith meets his friend Briggsy at a bar, orders a cheap Hamm's beer instead of Heineken, and deflects Briggsy's questions about his sister Sara's death, saying he doesn't want to talk about it.

scenes.heading: EXT. KEITH’S HOME - BROCKTON, MA - DUSK
scenes.summary: Keith jogs to the front door of his modest home in a working-class neighborhood.

scenes.heading: INT. KEITH’S HOME - KITCHEN - DUSK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Keith arrives home after his daughter's bedtime, feeling regret, and paces restlessly while washing dishes despite Caroline's suggestion to sit, asking if she thinks he's selfish.

scenes.heading: INT. KEITH'S HOME - BASEMENT - BROCKTON, MA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Keith goes to his basement office setup, puts on his cat t-shirt and red sweatband, sits in his gaming chair, and opens multiple tabs including stock charts, company reports, and his brokerage account showing his GameStop position.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - NURSING STATION - PITTSBURGH, PA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nurse Jenny Campbell watches Keith's YouTube video at a Pittsburgh hospital when an older patient in a gown bangs his walker against plexiglass, causing her to drop and smash her coffee cup.

scenes.heading: INT. BACK OFFICE - HOSPITAL - PITTSBURGH, PA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Chris bandages Jenny's bloody feet in a back office while they eat hospital food, and he mocks her for taking investment advice from a guy in a cat shirt, but Jenny defends Keith's thesis about Wall Street's rigged system.

scenes.heading: INT. COMMON ROOM - UNIVERSITY OF TEXAS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: College students Harmony and Riri play a drinking game in a dorm room, and when Harmony's prompt instructs her to put her hand down someone's pants, she chooses Riri, who stands and allows it while the group watches.

scenes.heading: INT. PUBLIC BUS - MOVING - DETROIT, MI - DAY
scenes.summary: Marcos Garcia rides a public bus in Detroit in November 2020, gazing out at shuttered stores and empty streets.

scenes.heading: EXT. DESERTED SHOPPING MALL - DETROIT, MI - DAY - LATER
scenes.summary: Marcos exits the bus at a deserted shopping mall and shuffles across the empty parking lot against cold wind.

scenes.heading: INT. DESERTED SHOPPING MALL - DETROIT, MI - DAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marcos enters the deserted mall where most stores are dark and padlocked, the food court empty, and the fountain off.

scenes.heading: INT. DESERTED SHOPPING MALL - GAMESTOP STORE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marcos unlocks the GameStop store, turns on the lights revealing a 90s time capsule interior, checks his bank account showing one hundred thirty-six dollars, and watches a Roaring Kitty video showing GME has tripled to thirteen ninety.

scenes.heading: INT. VOLVO STATION WAGON (MOVING) - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Kevin, Keith's brother, drives a Volvo station wagon while eating fries, cruises recklessly into another lane, spills a drink, and leans into the passenger seat to clean it up while still driving.

scenes.heading: INT. KEITH’S HOME - BASEMENT - BROCKTON, MA - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Keith broadcasts from his basement, explaining his GameStop thesis about asymmetric upside and betting on three specific scenarios including the company's potential reinvention.

scenes.heading: EXT. MANSION - BOSTON - LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Kevin delivers a DoorDash food bag to a mansion's front steps and calls out before returning to his car.

scenes.heading: INT. KEITH’S HOUSE - KITCHEN - BROCKTON, MA - LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Keith carries his two-year-old daughter in her highchair down to the basement, sets her across from his streaming setup with chicken tenders, and continues broadcasting while eating one himself.

scenes.heading: EXT. BE ACHFRONT MANSION - MIAMI, FL - DAY
scenes.summary: A beachfront mansion party in Miami features bikini-clad girls, rich guys, and trays of cocaine with no masks anywhere in December 2020.

scenes.heading: INT. GABE’S MODERN MANSION - BATHROOM - MIAMI, FL - DAY
scenes.summary: Gabe Plotkin takes Afrin nasal spray in his bathroom while on the phone with Steve Cohen, who mentions Ken Griffin's fifty-seven billion in short positions.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVE’S HOME - GREENWICH, CT - DAY
scenes.summary: Steve and Gabe discuss Ken Griffin over the phone, with Steve calling him an asshole for stealing analysts and overpaying for paintings, while Gabe receives a perfect lunch from his household staff and Steve feeds his pet pig Romeo.

scenes.heading: INT. GABE’S MODERN MANSION - KITCHEN - MIAMI, FL - DAY
scenes.summary: Gabe executes an order for six hundred thousand additional GameStop shorts, grooves to bass from the neighboring party, and agrees with Steve about taking dumb money, then GME bounces from three eighty-five to eighteen eighty-four by December thirty-first.
scenes.turningPointNote: Gabe massively increases his short position just as retail momentum builds, setting up his eventual catastrophic losses.

scenes.heading: EXT. KEITH’S HOUSE - BROCKTON, MA - ESTABLISHING - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Keith's house exterior shows modest Christmas decorations including a tinsel tree and one string of lights.

scenes.heading: INT. KEITH'S HOME - LIVING ROOM - BROCKTON, MA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The living room contains remnants of a New Year's celebration with empty champagne bottles.

scenes.heading: INT. KEITH'S HOME - BASEMENT - BROCKTON, MA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Keith broadcasts on New Year's Eve wearing a Santa hat, celebrating GameStop as a five-bagger since summer, showing his balance sheet proving he hasn't sold a single share, and getting emotional while revealing his sister Sara died in 2020.

scenes.heading: EXT. DORM - UNIVERSITY OF TEXAS - ESTABLISHING - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Establishing shot of a dorm building at the University of Texas at night.

scenes.heading: INT. HARMONY’S DORM ROOM - UNIVERSITY OF TEXAS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Harmony and Riri watch Keith's emotional New Year's broadcast in their dorm room, see he still holds all his shares, hear him reveal his sister's death, and Riri expresses wanting to hug him while Harmony jokes about keeping pants on.

scenes.heading: INT. ROBINHOOD HQ - MENLO PARK, CA - DAY
scenes.summary: Robinhood co-CEOs Vlad Tenev and Baiju Bhatt are interviewed by a journalist in their empty Menlo Park office, claiming Robinhood came from Occupy Wall Street and explaining their payment-for-order-flow business model while the journalist questions their narrative.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROBINHOOD HQ - GARAGE - MENLO PARK, CA - DAY
scenes.summary: Vlad and Baiju get into matching Teslas and Vlad plays Kendrick Lamar's Humble at maximum volume with bass shaking the seats as they drive away.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. - JENNY’S BANGED-UP CIVIC - MOVING - PITTSBURGH, PA
scenes.summary: Jenny drives her banged-up Honda Civic with her kids in the backseat, telling them not to repeat the bad words from the Kendrick Lamar song playing.

scenes.heading: EXT. UNIVERSITY OF TEXAS - DAY - SAME
scenes.summary: Harmony pedals across the University of Texas campus with a heavy backpack while Kendrick Lamar plays.

scenes.heading: INT. BUS - MOVING - DETROIT, MI - DAY - SAME
scenes.summary: Marcos rides the bus home in Detroit wearing a mask while Kendrick Lamar plays.

scenes.heading: INT. ESCALADE - MOVING - MIAMI, FL - DAY - SAME
scenes.summary: Gabe rides in an Escalade where the driver wears a mask but he doesn't while Kendrick Lamar plays.

scenes.heading: INT. FORD TAURUS - MOVING - BROCKTON, MA - DAY - SAME
scenes.summary: Keith rides in the cramped backseat of his parents' Ford Taurus with his brother Kevin, who raps along to Humble until their mother snaps at him to stop so their father can concentrate on driving.

scenes.heading: EXT. CALVARY CEMETERY - BROCKTON, MA - LATER THAT DAY
scenes.summary: The Gill family stands in heavy silence at Sara's grave in a cemetery with fresh pandemic graves visible in the distance, and Elaine begins sobbing as Keith and Kevin embrace her while their father takes a labored breath.

scenes.heading: INT. HARMONY’S DORM ROOM - UNIVERSIT Y OF TEXAS - DAY
scenes.summary: Harmony sees on her phone that Roaring Kitty just bought forty thousand more GameStop shares and tells Riri they need to buy more, suggesting selling foot pics, while Riri calls Ashton over to their dorm window.

scenes.heading: EXT. GAS STATION - PITTSBURGH, PA - DAY - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Jenny fills her gas tank while checking her phone and sees Keith now owns fifty thousand GME shares; her seven hundred shares bought at five eleven are now worth nearly fourteen thousand, over ten thousand in profit, and she feels confident until a handsome professional man pulls up and flirts with her.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOUR SEASONS - TENNIS COURT - PALM BEACH, FL - DAY
scenes.summary: Gabe plays tennis with Ken Griffin at the Four Seasons in Palm Beach, complaining about delays in demolishing his property for a tennis court and quoting his grandfather about refusing to borrow money.

scenes.heading: INT. SHOPPING MALL - GAMESTOP STORE - DETROIT, MI - DAY
scenes.summary: Marcos helps a teenage customer at GameStop, searching inventory meticulously for a particular game title, while Gabe's voiceover describes GameStop's six hundred thirty-two million dollar loss and casually imagines the company burning down.

scenes.heading: INT. COMMON ROOM - UNIVERSITY OF TEXAS - DAY
scenes.summary: A dozen college students gather around Riri's computer in a common room, drinking tequila and watching GME stock rise, unbuttoning clothing as part of a drinking game as the price hits $65 per share.

scenes.heading: INT. KEITH'S HOME - LIVING ROOM - BROCKTON, MA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Caroline sleeps on the sofa wearing a foam kitty mask surrounded by empty champagne bottles while Keith streams from the basement.

scenes.heading: INT. KEITH'S HOME - BASEMENT - BROCKTON, MA - DAY
scenes.summary: Keith streams drunk from his basement wearing a foam kitty mask, holding chicken tenders and celebrating a 90% GME increase in one day, howling and declaring they're going to the moon.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL MEETING ROOM - PITTSBURGH, PA - DAY
scenes.summary: Jenny tells Chris she's up $58,000 but refuses to sell GME because selling would be giving up and betraying everyone else, while Chris argues she should cash out and return to reality.

scenes.heading: INT. VOLVO (MOVING) - BROCKTON, MA - DAY
scenes.summary: Keith and Caroline drive with their baby while Kevin calls on speakerphone asking about buying lambos and diamonds, and Keith quickly says he won't cash out before remembering he hasn't discussed this with Caroline.

scenes.heading: INT. HARMONY’S DORM ROOM - UNIVERSITY OF TEXAS - DAY
scenes.summary: Riri sits in an empty kiddie pool recording a TikTok Live in a whisper, asking viewers whether Harmony should sell her GME stock.

scenes.heading: INT. LECTURE HALL - UNIVERSITY OF TEXAS - DAY
scenes.summary: Harmony watches Riri's livestream during a biochem lecture as commenters debate whether to sell, and Riri says only Roaring Kitty selling would convince Harmony to sell, then Harmony's AirPod falls out and her laptop plays the stream aloud.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGH SCHOOL TRACK - BROCKTON, MA - NIGHT - SAME
scenes.summary: Keith checks his E-Trade account showing $11 million in GME holdings at a high school track, stares at the SELL button, then puts his phone inside his shoe on the bleachers.

scenes.heading: INT. KEITH'S HOUSE - KITCHEN - BROCKTON, MA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Caroline reads aloud a post about future generations pointing to a GME chart and saying good men fought and died on this ground.

scenes.heading: INT. COMMON ROOM - DORM - UNIVERSITY OF TEXAS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Harmony reads to Riri a post describing the eternal combat between Lions and Hyenas since the spawn of the stock market.

scenes.heading: INT. JENNY’S HOUSE - KITCHEN - PITTSBURGH, PA - DAY
scenes.summary: Jenny dumps mac and cheese into boiling water while reading on her phone about Wall Street hedge funds having quants, billions, algorithms, and legal protection.

scenes.heading: EXT. BROCKTON STREET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Kevin sits beside his bike eating fries and reading a post asking what the working man has compared to Wall Street.

scenes.heading: INT. COMMON ROOM - UNIVERSITY OF TEXAS - DAY
scenes.summary: Students in a common room read a post over Harmony's shoulder stating that Wall Street literally calls retail investors 'dumb money.'

scenes.heading: INT. JENNY’S HOUSE - KITCHEN - PITTSBURGH, PA - DAY
scenes.summary: Jenny's finger hovers over the SELL button on her phone while mac and cheese boils over and burns, the smoke detector goes off, and she finally snaps out of it to climb on a chair.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. VARIOUS LOCATIONS - NIGHT - SAME
scenes.summary: Essential workers at various jobs ignore the world around them while debating whether to buy or sell GME stock.

scenes.heading: INT. DINING ROOM - GREENWICH - NIGHT - SAME
scenes.summary: Steve Cohen dines with friends attended by uniformed staff while a voiceover describes hedge fund guys eating wagyu steak with truffle shavings.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOUR SEASONS - POOL - PALM BEACH, FL - NIGHT - SAME
scenes.summary: Ken Griffin wades shirtless into a pool at the Four Seasons while a voiceover describes hedge fund managers frequenting strip clubs and having escorts on yacht parties.

scenes.heading: EXT. GABE’S MODERN MANSION - MIAMI, FL - NIGHT - SAME
scenes.summary: A chef serves shabbat dinner to Gabe, his wife Yaara, and their four children while a voiceover asks what hedge fund managers tell beautiful escorts.

scenes.heading: INT. COMMON ROOM - DORM - UNIVERSITY OF TEXAS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Riri stands holding a phone and shouts that hedge fund managers say 'dumb money babe' to escorts, and all the college kids join her shouting 'DUMB MONEY.'

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGH SCHOOL TRACK - BROCKTON, MA - NIGHT - SAME
scenes.summary: Keith runs a timed mile at the track, crosses seven seconds after the four-minute mark, slumps over frustrated at not beating his goal, then checks his phone again.

scenes.heading: INT. GABE’S MODERN MANSION - KITCHEN - MIAMI, FL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gabe stands at his kitchen sink doing dishes as water rushes from the faucet.

scenes.heading: INT. GABE’S MODERN MANSION - KIDS ROOM - MIAMI, FL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gabe sits on the edge of his 10-year-old son's bed after finishing bedtime stories and shares a sweet hug and kiss.

scenes.heading: INT. GABE’S MODERN MANSION - BEDROOM - MIAMI, FL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gabe and Yaara lie in bed together discussing their week, and Gabe smugly dismisses the short squeeze threat, predicting the dam will break next week when people cash out and the whole thing comes crashing down.

scenes.heading: EXT. 6342 NORTH BAY ROAD - MIAMI, FL - DAY
scenes.summary: Gabe walks across the expansive grounds toward his other mansion.

scenes.heading: INT. 6342 NORTH BAY ROAD - MIAMI, FL - DAY
scenes.summary: Gabe strolls through his new mansion when Steve Cohen calls about idiots and GME, Gabe says he'll dial in, then panicked, hangs up and runs.

scenes.heading: EXT. GABE’S MODERN MANSION - MIAMI, FL - DAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gabe runs into his other mansion as news anchors report GameStop up 51%, 89%, and 144%.

scenes.heading: INT. KEITH'S HOME - BASEMENT - BROCKTON, MA - DAY - SAME
scenes.summary: Keith sits wide-eyed and speechless in his basement as Caroline calls from another room asking if everything is okay.

scenes.heading: INT. PSYCH WARD - PITTSBURGH, PA - DAY - SAM E
scenes.summary: Jenny stands at work in a psych ward, staring agape at a communal TV broadcasting CNBC and saying 'Holy fucking shit.'

scenes.heading: INT. DORM ROOM - UNIVERSITY OF TEXAS - DAY
scenes.summary: Harmony and Riri watch a CNBC clip on TikTok in their pajamas, Harmony says 'Holy fucking shit' which startles and wakes their roommate.

scenes.heading: INT. SHOPPING MALL - GAMESTOP STORE - DETROIT - DAY - SAME
scenes.summary: Marcos sits behind a GameStop checkout desk, loads the stock price, and shocked, loses his balance and falls to the ground.

scenes.heading: INT. VLAD’S HOUSE - BATHROOM - MENLO PARK, CA - DAY - SAME
scenes.summary: Vlad freezes mid-shave in a towel with a razor against his neck while watching CNBC and saying 'HOLY FUCKING...'

scenes.heading: INT. FOUR SEASONS - DINING ROOM - PALM BEACH, FL - DAY
scenes.summary: Ken Griffin shouts 'SHITBALLS' after getting a spot of ketchup on his vest, not about GME.

scenes.heading: INT. GABE'S MODERN MANSION - OFFICE - MIAMI, FL - DAY
scenes.summary: Gabe stares wide-eyed at his screen in disbelief.

scenes.heading: INT. KEITH’S HOME - BASEMENT - BROCKTON, MA - DAY
scenes.summary: Keith sits in front of a GME stock chart clutching a stuffed kitty and tells Caroline that the short squeeze is happening, then a supercut shows news coverage of GameStop soaring to record highs as retail traders wield unprecedented power.
scenes.turningPointNote: The short squeeze Keith predicted begins in real time, validating his thesis and triggering the central conflict with Wall Street hedge funds.

scenes.heading: INT. DORM ROOM - UNIVERSITY OF TEXAS - DAY
scenes.summary: Harmony and Riri lie in bed together staring at an iPhone, Harmony says she can't look, Riri refreshes and reports it's up another 4%, and they debate whether to sell.

scenes.heading: INT. VARIOUS ROOMS - WORLDWIDE - DAY/NIGHT - SAME
scenes.summary: Marcos, Jenny, and essential workers watch news with fingers on the SELL button, then discover Elon Musk tweeted 'Gamestonk!!' which holds them back from selling.

scenes.heading: INT. KEITH'S HOME - BASEMENT - BROCKTON, MA - DAY
scenes.summary: Keith and Caroline watch TV in the basement with their sleeping baby as CNBC reports GME is up 581% in three months and could take out hedge fund firms including Gabe Plotkin's, Caroline asks how much they made today and Keith says $5 million.

scenes.heading: INT. GABE’S MODERN MANSION - BEDROOM - MIAMI, FL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Yaara works a crossword as Gabe gets into bed, she reaches for his limp hand and asks how much they lost today, and Gabe says a billion for both today and yesterday.

scenes.heading: EXT. GABE’S MODERN MANSION - MIAMI, FL - ESTABLISHING
scenes.summary: Establishing shot of Gabe's modern mansion in Miami.

scenes.heading: INT. GABE’S MODERN MANSION - BATHROOM - MIAMI, FL - DAY
scenes.summary: A make-up artist applies powder to Gabe's face as he sits in a suit absorbed in his phone watching the GME stock chart jackknife upward, tiny beads of sweat appearing on his brow despite his calm exterior.

scenes.heading: INT. GABE’S MODERN MANSION - OFFICE - MIAMI, FL - DAY
scenes.summary: Gabe sits at his desk watching GME climb while his phone rings ignored, toggles between the stock chart and a spreadsheet as pressure builds, then finally calls someone and orders them to get out of all their GME positions now.
scenes.turningPointNote: Gabe capitulates and closes out Melvin Capital's short position, marking the hedge fund's defeat and massive financial loss.

scenes.heading: EXT. STEVE’S HOUSE - GREENWICH, CONNECTICUT - SAME
scenes.summary: Steve watches workmen unload items when Gabe calls, Steve answers smiling and guesses Gabe needs cash.

scenes.heading: INT. GABE’S MODERN MANSION - OFFICE - MIAMI, FL - DAY - SAME
scenes.summary: Gabe hesitates when Steve asks if he called Ken first, admits Phillips reached out, and Steve offers to buy in and let Ken fill in the rest, calling Ken a prick.

scenes.heading: INT. FOUR SEASONS RESORT - DINING ROOM - PALM BEACH, FL - DAY
scenes.summary: Ken's phone rings and he answers, hearing Gabe struggle to speak.

scenes.heading: INT. GABE’S MODERN MANSION - OFFICE - MIAMI, FL - DAY - SAME
scenes.summary: Gabe can't get the words out due to shame, finally says he may need to borrow money, Ken immediately says it's done and tells him to keep moving forward, then hangs up laughing and calling Gabe a poor guy.

scenes.heading: INT. JENNY’S HOUSE - KITCHEN - PITTSBURGH, PA - DAY
scenes.summary: Jenny runs around her kitchen multitasking while Chris waits with car keys as CNBC reports Citadel and Point72 infused $3 billion into Melvin, and Jenny rants about the unfairness of hedge funds getting bailouts while she got one $600 check.

scenes.heading: INT. KEITH’S CHILDHOOD HOME - BROCKTON, MA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Kevin stares at Jenny's post during family dinner, reveals Keith is worth $23 million and refusing to sell, Keith's parents react with shock and disbelief, his mother screams and nearly faints, and his father demands Keith sell immediately.

scenes.heading: INT. LECTURE HALL - UNIVERSITY OF TEXAS - DAY
scenes.summary: Harmony and Riri whisper in the back of a lecture hall debating whether to sell, Riri asks what Roaring Kitty is doing, and Harmony says he's worth $47.973 million and believes no amount of money is worth more than the pleasure of something.

scenes.heading: INT. FOUR SEASONS - PALM BEACH, FL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A letter slides under Ken Griffin's door at the Four Seasons, he opens it to reveal a $25 GameStop gift card.

scenes.heading: EXT. GABE'S MODERN MANSION - MIAMI, FL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gabe walks to his front gate which opens to reveal a bucket of Kentucky Fried Chicken ordered by THE TENDIEMAN, and Yaara comes out asking who it's from.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - NURSING STATION - PITTSBURGH, PA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jenny packs her desk at the hospital and tells Chris she's going to Florida first class on her Visa, not selling her GameStop stock, prompting Chris to remove his mask in shock.

scenes.heading: INT. MARCOS’S PARENTS’ HOME - KITCHEN - DETROIT, MI - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Marcos unloads groceries while watching his phone and tells his skeptical parents in Spanish that he has $175,000 in GameStop stock and could buy them a house, but his father dismisses the 8 million WSB holders who can't spell 'hold' right.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - UNIVERSITY OF TEXAS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Students in a dorm hallway react with confusion and outrage to something on a laptop, while Harmony slams her door and asks Riri what they should do as Riri tries unsuccessfully to load Wall Street Bets.

scenes.heading: INT. KEITH’S HOME - BASEMENT - BROCKTON, MA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Keith tells Caroline that Wall Street Bets has been shut down for 'hateful and discriminatory content' the same day GME hit its high, and Caroline notices his E-Trade balance is close to $50 million as his phone keeps ringing unanswered.
scenes.turningPointNote: Wall Street Bets is shut down at the peak of the GME rally, cutting off communication between retail investors and creating uncertainty about whether the stock will tank.

scenes.heading: INT. DORM ROOM - UNIVERSITY OF TEXAS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Harmony and Riri hover their fingers over the SELL buttons on their Robinhood accounts, unable to see Roaring Kitty's balance sheet with WSB down, debating whether to sell their $150,000 in shares and options before it goes to zero.

scenes.heading: INT. KEITH’S HOME - NURSERY - BROCKTON, MA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Keith paces with his crying baby while Caroline watches, and when he checks his phone Wall Street Bets still won't load; Caroline asks if they'll talk about this, and Keith slows his pacing and hums a lullaby that sounds like the GameStop theme.

scenes.heading: INT. DORM ROOM - UNIVERSITY OF TEXAS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Harmony and Riri lie awake in bed feeling sick, holding hands, and Riri promises they'll buy the stock back first thing in the morning.

scenes.heading: INT. MODERN MANSION - MENLO PARK, CA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vlad attends a late-night party in violation of Covid restrictions, tries to flirt with a hot girl he can't hear over the music, and points out Elon Musk across the room while declaring this is the center of the world.

scenes.heading: INT. ROBINHOOD SECURITIES - OFFICE - LAKE MARY, FLORIDA
scenes.summary: Norman Ashkenas calls Vlad at the party to tell him the NSCC is requesting a $3 billion deposit to clear trades, and Vlad sobers up when he realizes Robinhood has only ever raised $2 billion total.
scenes.turningPointNote: Robinhood receives an impossible $3 billion deposit requirement from the DTCC, creating a liquidity crisis that will force them to restrict trading.

scenes.heading: EXT. JENNY’S HOUSE - PI TTSBURGH, PA - DAWN
scenes.summary: Jenny gets into an Uber at dawn, checking the GME chart as it climbs from $265 to $483, and tries to access Wall Street Bets but it still won't load.

scenes.heading: EXT. BUS STOP - DETROIT, MI - DAWN - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Marcos waits for the bus in freezing temperatures and opens Robinhood to find the BUY option for GME is grayed out, leaving only SELL available.
scenes.turningPointNote: Robinhood removes the ability to buy GameStop, only allowing users to sell, fundamentally changing the dynamics of the short squeeze.

scenes.heading: INT. DORM ROOM - UNIVERSITY OF TEXAS - DAY
scenes.summary: Harmony and Riri discover Robinhood has grayed out the BUY option for GME, and Harmony realizes this is the short squeeze moment where hedge funds are buying to cover while Robinhood stops retail traders from competing for shares.

scenes.heading: INT. BAIJU BHATT’S APARTMENT - BEDROOM - SAN FRANCISCO - DAY
scenes.summary: Baiju feeds his baby while looking at Robinhood's one-star App Store rating and yells at Vlad over the phone for shutting down trading on GameStop.

scenes.heading: INT. VLAD’S HOUSE - KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Vlad makes a smoothie and tells Baiju the DTCC lowered the deposit requirement to $700 million if he shut off buying, so he did, and argues people will get more pissed if they can't sell when the price tanks.

scenes.heading: INT. BARSTOOL SPORTS - OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Dave Portnoy interviews Vlad on Zoom and tells him everyone watching hates his guts, while Vlad awkwardly laughs and says he's glad to be on the show.

scenes.heading: INT. ROBINHOOD HQ - MENLO PARK, CA - DAY
scenes.summary: Vlad wears a 'Taco Tuesday' cap and tells Elon Musk in an interview that Robinhood had no liquidity problem, then tells CNN the decision to restrict trading was not made on behalf of market makers or hedge funds.

scenes.heading: INT. BARSTOOL SPORTS - OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Dave Portnoy gives a blistering rant on CNN saying there's no rational explanation for Robinhood's actions without outside pressure, while CNN displays tweets from Elizabeth Warren and AOC criticizing the situation.

scenes.heading: INT. DORM ROOM - UNIVERSITY OF TEXAS - DAY
scenes.summary: Harmony and Riri film a TikTok calling Vlad an obfuscating piece of shit and yelling together 'WHERE MY PUSSY AT,' while Vlad jogs past angry protestors toward Robinhood HQ and gets pelted with eggs.

scenes.heading: INT. MARCOS’S PARENTS’ HOME - KITCHEN - DETROIT, MI - DAY
scenes.summary: Marcos watches the news with his parents as a TV anchor reports that Robinhood's decision to block buying triggered a panic sell-off and GameStop shares have plummeted.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL BAR - FLORIDA - DUSK
scenes.summary: Jenny stares at her phone in rage at a hotel bar in Florida, her finger hovering over the BUY and SELL buttons as the news reports the rally is ending.

scenes.heading: INT. LIBRARY - UNIVERSITY OF TEXAS - SAME
scenes.summary: Riri gets a covid test while Harmony notices Keith on TV in the reception area and yells that their boy is on the news, causing Riri to spin around with the swab still in her nose and trigger a nosebleed.

scenes.heading: INT. GABE’S MODERN MANSION - MIAMI, FL - DAY - SAME
scenes.summary: Gabe sits on the couch with his wife and kids and looks up in terror at the TV showing Keith's face as the news reports Keith resigned from MassMutual last week.

scenes.heading: INT. LIBRARY - UNIVERSITY OF TEXAS - DAY
scenes.summary: Harmony and Riri watch the news with toilet paper in Riri's nose as the anchor reports Keith has repeatedly said he has no comment at this time.

scenes.heading: EXT. KEITH’S HOME - BEDROOM - BROCKTON, MA - DAY - SAME
scenes.summary: Keith pulls up to find reporters on his front lawn, waves awkwardly at them including some fans, and walks over to sign an autograph but is instead served with a congressional subpoena.
scenes.turningPointNote: Keith is served with a congressional subpoena, escalating the situation from online phenomenon to legal and political crisis.

scenes.heading: INT. KEITH’S HOUSE - KITCHEN - BROCKTON, MA - DAY
scenes.summary: Caroline paces while Keith sits buried in analytics, and she confronts him about the congressional subpoena, pointing out reporters are on their lawn and the game has changed, but Keith keeps staring at his charts.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGH SCHOOL TRACK - BROCKTON, MA - DAY
scenes.summary: Keith jogs on a high school track practicing his testimony when Kevin appears with a box of Nike Zooms, and they talk about their dad calling Kevin in tears; Kevin tells Keith to 'run naked' as a metaphor for taking this head-on.

scenes.heading: EXT. KEITH’S HOUSE - KITCHEN - BROCKTON, MA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Caroline works in the yard ignoring the reporters while Keith shows her his new shoes and explains Kevin's 'run naked' metaphor; she asks what he'll do and he says he doesn't know, then she tells him to go inside and write his testimony.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. DORM ROOM - UNIVERSITY OF TEXAS - DAY
scenes.summary: Harmony and Riri are locked out of their dorm room and see Keith posted a GIF of a cat driving a motorcycle, realizing he held despite losing $30 million; they decide to buy back in at $109 and Harmony taps BUY, saying 'This is for you, Dad.'

scenes.heading: INT. KEITH'S HOME - BASEMENT - BROCKTON, MA - DAY
scenes.summary: Keith watches the news announce he and executives from Robinhood, Citadel, and Melvin Capital will testify at a congressional hearing next month, and he looks terrified.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGH SCHOOL TRACK - BROCKTON, MA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Keith runs loops on the track at night, practicing different versions of his opening statement and repeatedly failing to find the right words.

scenes.heading: INT. NEWS STUDIO - DAY
scenes.summary: A TV anchor reports GameStop shares have begun climbing back up after Robinhood restored the buy option and retail investors refuse to let the stock go, with GME back up to $109.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPLANE - FLORIDA - DAY
scenes.summary: Jenny boards a flight home considerably poorer, drops into a first-class seat she can no longer afford, and tells the flight attendant she lost over half a million dollars in GameStop when Wall Street cheated, laughing at herself for not cashing out.

scenes.heading: INT. DESERTED SHOPPING MALL - GAMESTOP STORE - DETROIT - DAY
scenes.summary: Marcos rings up a transaction at the bustling GameStop store and tells Brad he won't push pre-owned games or do the Circle of Life bullshit anymore, but he's not quitting because he likes the stock.

scenes.heading: INT. GABE’S MODERN MANSION - OFFICE - MIAMI, FL - DAY
scenes.summary: A team of lawyers and PR operatives prep Gabe for his testimony, telling him to remove references to his dad being an executive and to say 'a good college' instead of Northwestern.

scenes.heading: INT. GABE’S MODERN MANSION - OFFICE - MIAMI, FL - DAY
scenes.summary: Gabe shows the lawyers and PR team his oceanfront office and they tell him it's too bright, too blue, and not a great idea for optics.

scenes.heading: INT. FOUR SEASONS - BAR - PALM BEACH, FL - DAY
scenes.summary: Ken Griffin is about to tee off when lawyers approach with a congressional subpoena specifically requesting him, not Mecane, and they want to discuss finding a less opulent backdrop for his Zoom testimony.

scenes.heading: INT. GABE’S OTHER MANSION - MIAMI, FL - DAY
scenes.summary: Gabe shows the lawyers a dingy room with bad lighting and cheap Venetian blinds, and they approve it for his testimony backdrop.

scenes.heading: INT. KEITH’S HOME - LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Keith's war council of Kevin, Briggsy, and Caroline run a mock hearing with pizza and beer, grilling Keith with rapid-fire questions while drunk and mocking him, as Keith calmly provides prepared answers but struggles when they interrupt and talk over him.

scenes.heading: INT. KEITH'S HOME - HALL BATH - BROCKTON, MA - DAY
scenes.summary: Keith tries to tie his tie in the bathroom but his hands are shaking too much, so Caroline does it for him and tells him he'll be great, though she knows he's right that if it goes bad it will be real bad.

scenes.heading: INT. GABE’S NEW MANSION - MIAMI, FL - DAY
scenes.summary: Gabe settles into his seat and tests his microphone for the congressional hearing.

scenes.heading: INT. FOUR SEASONS - CONFERENCE ROOM - PALM BEACH, FL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Ken takes his seat at a conference table while a lawyer adjusts his ring light, with four lawyers positioned at the far end of the table.

scenes.heading: INT. KEITH'S HOME - BASEMENT - BROCKTON, MA - DAY
scenes.summary: Keith sits nervously in his gaming chair, checks his appearance with his tie and combed hair, then adds his red headband to the wall next to the HANG IN THERE cat poster and begins his testimony thanking the committee.

scenes.heading: INT. KEITH’S CHILDHOOD HOME - BROCKTON, MA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Steve and Elaine watch Keith on screen with Kevin as Keith says 'I am not a cat,' and Elaine asks what he means by that.

scenes.heading: INT. COMMON ROOM - UNIVERSITY OF TEXAS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: College kids play a drinking game doing shots every time Keith says 'cat,' while Harmony and Riri watch stony-faced and serious as Keith testifies he's not an institutional investor or hedge fund.

scenes.heading: INT. PATIENT ROOM - PITTSBURGH, PA - DAY
scenes.summary: Jenny cares for an elderly patient while the in-room TV plays Keith's testimony that he doesn't have clients, doesn't provide personalized investment advice, and is just an individual whose GameStop investment was based on his own research.

scenes.heading: INT. KEITH’S CHILDHOOD HOME - BROCKTON, MA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Keith testifies that he grew up in a non-wealthy family in Brockton with a truck driver father and nurse mother, and was the first in his family to earn a four-year college degree in 2009.

scenes.heading: INT. GAMESTOP STORE - DETROIT - DAY
scenes.summary: Marcos wears a red headband in homage and watches Keith testify on his phone about being unemployed for significant periods from 2010-2017, using that time to educate himself about investing, and explaining he shared his ideas to help others and accept critiques.

scenes.heading: INT. GABE’S NEW MANSION - MIAMI, FL - DAY
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. VLAD’S HOUSE - MENLO PARK, CA - DAY
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. FOUR SEASONS - OFFICE - PALM BEACH, FL - DAY
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. MAHOGANY-PANELED STUDY - GREENWICH, CONNECTICUT - DAY
scenes.summary: Steve Cohen watches Ken Griffin's congressional testimony on C-SPAN while being stretched on his massage table and calls him a prick.

scenes.heading: INT. FOUR SEASONS - OFFICE - PALM BEACH, FL - DAY
scenes.summary: Ken Griffin testifies to Congress that Citadel had no role in Robinhood's decision to restrict trading, denying any conversation about preventing GameStop purchases.

scenes.heading: INT. VLAD’S HOUSE - MENLO PARK, CA - DAY
scenes.summary: A Congresswoman confronts Vlad Tenev about harming customers and asks if he'll pass payment-for-order-flow proceeds to Robinhood users, which he deflects and refuses.

scenes.heading: INT. KEITH’S HOME - BASEMENT - BROCKTON, MA - DAY
scenes.summary: A Congressman questions Keith about predicting GameStop's movements and suggests he had inside information, which Keith denies while making eye contact with Caroline for strength.

scenes.heading: INT. COMMON ROOM - UNIVERSITY OF TEXAS - DAY
scenes.summary: Riri and Harmony discuss whether Keith will sell his GameStop shares after his testimony, with Riri hoping they never have to find out.

scenes.heading: INT. KEITH’S HOUSE - BASEMENT - BROCKTON, MA - DAY
scenes.summary: Keith finishes testifying and loosens his tie while Caroline asks if it went well.

scenes.heading: INT. KEITH’S HOUSE - BROCKTON, MA - ESTABLISHING - DAWN
scenes.summary: Establishing shot of Keith's house at dawn.

scenes.heading: INT. KEITH’S HOUSE - BEDROOM - BROCKTON, MA - DAWN
scenes.summary: Keith slips out of bed early while Caroline and the baby sleep and heads downstairs.

scenes.heading: INT. KEITH’S HOUSE - BASEMENT - BROCKTON, MA - DAY
scenes.summary: Keith exercises his options to buy 50,000 more GameStop shares instead of selling, then uploads his updated balance sheet to WallStreetBets.
scenes.turningPointNote: Keith makes his final decisive move, doubling down on GameStop after testimony rather than cashing out, cementing his commitment and legend

scenes.heading: EXT. KEITH'S HOUSE - BROCKTON, MA - DAY
scenes.summary: Kevin discovers a red Porsche convertible parked across the street with a note from Keith reading 'Love, Kitty' attached to the car keys.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGH SCHOOL TRACK - BROCKTON, MA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Keith and Kevin race naked on a high school track at night as title cards reveal Keith's final post was worth $34 million and he has retreated from public life.
scenes.turningPointNote: The film's epilogue reveals Keith's final outcome and permanent withdrawal from the public eye, closing his arc
